acceptance_note: Three SUDS tiers require two stored bits per mapped column.
Two fixed-point thresholds per layer are enough for KEEP/DEGRADE/PRUNE.
Arrival, deadline, and slack fields are modeled as retained scheduler metadata.
Two threshold comparisons per mapped column are modeled as scheduler-side logic.
The runtime sideband can carry compact tier IDs rather than full slack arrays.
Validity tags prevent stale tier maps from being reused across unmatched profiles.
Static tier and timing metadata are near 4 KiB for the promoted MobileViT-S profile.
Matches the 3% nominal control term already included in P0.4 sensitivity.
Matches the 10% stress control term already included in P0.4 sensitivity.
One scheduler compare slot per layer is less than 0.002% of summed modeled serial cycles.
